title: IPv6 and Modern Programmable Networks · Operations Brief: Net Infrastructure Code
summary: Extend the branch design for IPv6, centralized policy, overlays, zero trust, SASE, and infrastructure as code. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net infrastructure code increment in a dual-stack and programmable-network transition plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Manage network templates, playbooks, inventories, source control, review, conflict, drift, upgrade, and rollback evidence.
Relate zero-trust policy decisions, least privilege, continuous context, SASE, and SSE service boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Predict Net Infrastructure Code
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: network ipv6 modern. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for manage network templates, playbooks, inventories, source control, review, conflict, drift, upgrade, and rollback evidence.

steps.title: Build Net Infrastructure Code evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-infrastructure-code-1t6jc7" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Infrastructure Code into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Defend the Net Infrastructure Code model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Infrastructure Code applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
